faq.question: How much does window replacement cost in Sunnyvale?
faq.answer: Sunnyvale homeowners typically invest $7,000 to $16,000 for a full window replacement project covering 10–16 windows. This Silicon Valley tech hub — home to major tech companies along North Mathilda Avenue — features Eichler mid-century moderns, 1960s–1980s ranches, and newer condo developments. Eichler floor-to-ceiling glass systems require specialized installation. Single window installation starts at approximately $590.

faq.question: Do I need a permit for window replacement in Sunnyvale?
faq.answer: Yes. Sunnyvale requires a building permit for window replacements. Our team handles all permit applications with the City of Sunnyvale Building Division on your behalf.

faq.question: Which window brands does Deluxe Windows install in Sunnyvale?
faq.answer: Sunnyvale's tech-oriented Silicon Valley market drives demand for energy-efficient, high-performance products. We most frequently install Anlin Del Mar triple-pane for maximum solar heat gain control in the valley's warm climate, Andersen 400-Series for Eichler and mid-century homes, and Milgard Tuscany vinyl for cost-effective whole-home replacements.

faq.question: Does Deluxe Windows serve all Sunnyvale neighborhoods?
faq.answer: Yes — we serve all Sunnyvale neighborhoods including Lakewood Village, Murphy Avenue historic district, Birdland, Ticonderoga, and all zip codes: 94085, 94086, 94087, 94089.
